#  What are the different types of bank accounts in Procore Pay? 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
##  Answer 
With Procore Pay, the Payor and Payee roles require different types of bank accounts to send and receive payments. See [How do funds flow between bank accounts with Procore Pay?](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/how-do-funds-flow)
#####  Important
**In Procore Pay, all bank accounts must be verified to send or receive payments.** See [What is a verified bank account in Procore Pay?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-a-verified-bank-account-in-procore-pay)
This table details the different types of required bank accounts for Procore Pay.  
| **Funding Account**  | Payor  |  In Procore Pay, a _funding account_ is a bank account where money is held by a financial institution and controlled by the payor. This is the 'Pay From' account. **  
**
* * *
**  
How does it work?** After the account owner authorizes the disbursement in Procore Pay, the financial institution for the deposit account:
  * Submits a 'drawdown' request to the funding account for the disbursement amount.
  * Withdraws the disbursement amount from the funding account. This is the 'drawdown of funds.'

 | 
  * This is a bank account that you open with your financial institution.
  * You can add one (1) or more funding accounts.
  * A funding account must be mapped to a deposit account.

 | [Add Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts)  |  
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| **Deposit Account**  | Payor  |  In Procore Pay, a _deposit account_ is a bank account where money is held by a financial institution to process the payor's authorized transactions. This is the transaction banking account. **  
**
* * *
  
**How does it work?** After the drawdown of funds, the deposit account:
  * Transmits the authorized disbursement amount into the deposit account to complete the 'drawdown of funds.'
  * Processes the authorized payment orders for each invoice in the disbursement.
  * Withdraws each invoice payment from the deposit account.

 | 
  * You must open at least one (1) deposit account during the Procore Pay implementation process.
  * At least one (1) deposit account is required to add a funding account to Procore
  * You can open one (1) or more deposit accounts.

 | [Open a Deposit Account as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/open-deposit-accounts) [Add Funding Accounts as a Payor](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/add-funding-accounts)  |  
| **Beneficiary Account**  | Payee  |  In Procore Pay, a _beneficiary account_ is a bank account where money is held by a financial institution and controlled by the payee. This is the 'Pay To' bank account. **  
**
* * *
  
**How does it work?** After the invoice payment is withdrawn from the deposit account, each payee on the disbursement:
  * Receives their invoice payment as a deposit in their beneficiary account.
  * This is the payee's invoice payment that completes the payment transaction.

 | 
  * For payees, a beneficiary account is termed a _bank account._
  * For payors, it is termed a _beneficiary account_.
  * Payees can add multiple beneficiary accounts to their Procore account.
  * Payees can only designate one (1) bank account to receive payments.

 | [Add a Bank Account as a Payee](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payee-setup-guide/add-your-bank-account) [Edit a Bank Account as a Payee](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/edit-a-bank-account-as-a-payee)  |  
## See Also
  * [What is a verified bank account in Procore Pay?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-a-verified-bank-account-in-procore-pay)
  * [How do funds flow between bank accounts with Procore Pay?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-do-funds-flow-between-bank-accounts-with-procore-pay)
  * [How do you add bank accounts to Procore Pay?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-how-do-you-add-bank-accounts-to-procore-pay)


In Procore Pay, a _payor_ is a general contractor who sends invoice payments to specialty contractors.
In Procore Pay, a _payee_ is a specialty contractor who receives invoice payments from a general contractor. Also called a _beneficiary_.
title: What are the different types of bank accounts in Procore Pay?
description: Describes the different types of bank accounts in Procore Pay.
